items.title: Fix ms_download.py file detection — eliminate push_files.py manual step

items.title: Fix scoring weights automation
items.note: Changing weight sliders on Equity Scout and Market Screener required 2 clicks to rescore.  Let's have it rescore after the new weights are confirmed

items.title: Remove or demote Gross Margin from composite scoring
items.note: Gross margin is too context-dependent to be a universal moat indicator. High GM businesses with no moat (Groupon 90% GM, strong buy) and low GM businesses with strong moats (Costco 13% GM, 46 score) both score incorrectly. Remove from composite or limit to intra-industry comparison only.

items.title: Restructure Debt/FCF — use outstanding principal and cash available for debt service
items.note: CADS-based debt metric is built and live: evaluate_buffett_funnel() in sec_utils.py computes Cash Available for Debt Service (Operating Income + D&A - Capex) and runs a dual debt hurdle in parallel -- simple (Debt/Net Income) and refined (Debt/CADS) -- passing on EITHER, which is exactly the "do not penalize structural float users like insurers" goal from the original note. Live and validated in Market Screener (debt_hurdle_cleared shows which one cleared per company). REMAINING: Dashboard and Equity Scout still score debt with the old debt_to_fcf metric via score_stock_breakdown()/DEFAULT_WEIGHTS -- they have not adopted the CADS-based dual-hurdle logic. Next step is porting it there, or deciding whether Dashboard/Equity Scout should move toward the pass/fail funnel style entirely (see #34, same situation). UPDATE (this session): Dual-hurdle CADS fix ported to the canonical score_stock_breakdown() (sec_utils.py) -- Debt/FCF criterion now scores off whichever of debt_to_fcf/debt_to_cads is lower, same philosophy as the Market Screener funnel. Also ported into Equity Scout's local score_stock() duplicate. Dashboard and Compare Stocks import the canonical scorer directly, so they inherited the fix automatically. All three pages now use the CADS dual-hurdle. Closing.

items.title: Demote FCF Yield and Price/Owner Earnings to secondary screens
items.note: Both are valuation metrics — having both in composite double-weights relative value. Great businesses (V, MA) will always look expensive. Per Munger: better to buy great business at fair price than fair business at great price. Move both to secondary screen: quality first, then check if price is reasonable

items.title: Overhaul ROIC — historical 10-year cash accounting basis
items.note: Current ROIC uses single-period accrual accounting. Replace with 10-year historical trend, cash accounting view (owners earnings equivalent). Any company returning >12% after-tax ROIC for 10+ years almost certainly has a durable competitive advantage. Heavily increase default weight.

items.title: Fix interest coverage — use interest paid and cash available for debt service
items.note: Replace interest expense (accrual) with interest paid (cash). Replace EBITDA/earnings denominator with cash available for debt service. Add capitalized interest check. Express as margin (% interest paid to cash available for debt service) alongside principal multiple.

items.title: Add financial firm detection — flag for alternative scoring
items.note: Bank + insurer alt scoring is built and live (#36 v1, scoped to Buffett/Berkshire's own playbook per owner's choice — Market Screener now runs banks/insurers through their own funnel + scorer instead of hard-excluding them). New EDGAR concept mappings added (interest income/expense, noninterest income/expense, loan loss provision, premiums earned, policyholder benefits, underwriting expense — all verified against real JPM/Progressive filings before use). New derived metrics: ROE, Equity/Assets (capital cushion), Net Interest Margin (proxy — EDGAR has no average-earning-assets tag), Efficiency Ratio, Provision/NI, Combined Ratio — computed for both latest period and full history. evaluate_financial_firm_funnel() (pass/fail gate: 10yr avg ROE, capital cushion, subtype quality leg, no dilution) and score_financial_firm_breakdown() (weighted 0-100 score) live in sec_utils.py, wired into Market Screener's fetch/worker/debug-tool/result-cards/CSV export. Validated with a synthetic 6-year bank + insurer dataset run through the real code path (not reimplemented logic) before pushing. REMAINING: (1) other financial SIC codes — brokers, REITs, real estate, investment offices — still just excluded by the skip toggle, no alt framework yet (each needs its own metric set: AUM-based for brokers, FFO-based for REITs); (2) not yet wired into Equity Scout / Compare Stocks / Dashboard, only Market Screener so far — those pages will still show bank/insurer tickers under the standard (inapplicable) framework until ported. UPDATE (July 2026, post-deploy validation): ran the actual live Market Screener scan (88 real bank/insurer survivors) against the shipped thresholds and found the insurer Equity/Assets and Combined Ratio 'great' tiers were miscalibrated — reused bank-appropriate capital thresholds (8%/10%) that insurers structurally clear 3-4x over (they don't take deposits), so 20 of 33 real insurer survivors (61%) scored a flat 100/100 regardless of actual differences in capitalization. Fixed: added separate insurance-specific Equity/Assets thresholds (good 15%/great 25%, vs bank's 8%/10%) to both score_financial_firm_breakdown() and the funnel's capital gate, and tightened Combined Ratio from 95%/100% to 85%/95% (still standard industry benchmarks, just not clustered at the edge). Re-validated against the real survivor data — weaker names (CNO Financial, Elevance) now correctly land in 'good'/'weak' tiers instead of maxing out. KNOWN REMAINING LIMITATION: monoline mortgage/credit insurers (MGIC, Radian, Essent, NMI, Enact — SIC 6351, same bucket as classic P&C/title insurance) run a structurally different balance sheet (58-78% equity/assets, single-digit combined ratios by business-model design, not a comparable risk profile) and will still score very high under any P&C-calibrated threshold — a genuine apples-to-oranges problem within the 'insurance' bucket, not a threshold-tuning issue. Splitting mortgage/credit insurers into their own subtype is real further work, tracked under #70. FOLLOW-UP (same session): rather than leave monoline mortgage/credit insurers scoring near-perfect under mismatched P&C thresholds, moved SIC 6351 (Surety Insurance — MGIC, Radian, Essent, NMI, Enact, financial-guaranty insurers) out of INSURANCE_SIC_CODES entirely. They now classify as "other_financial" and are excluded by the Market Screener's skip toggle by default, same as brokers/REITs/real estate, until #70 builds them their own subtype.

items.title: Add cyclical firm detection — flag for full-cycle analysis caveat
items.note: Current model uses latest year data — makes it a momentum scout for cyclicals. Deere, Warrior Met Coal, CF Industries, ADM all score based on where they are in the cycle not intrinsic value. Detect cyclicals and flag that single-period scoring is unreliable. Full-cycle ROIC analysis needed.

items.title: Add scoring transparency — show raw data driving each metric
items.note: Users cannot see what underlying financial data is being pulled or how each metric is calculated. Add expandable detail panel showing raw inputs (revenue, FCF, debt figures etc.) and how they map to each score component. Critical for trust and debugging.

items.title: Add operating metrics panel to Equity Scout
items.note: Add supplemental panel showing: working capital ratios, operating cash profile, cash conversion as % of revenue, FCF yield as % of revenue (not market cap). Goal: efficiency view of operations independent of market valuation. Normalise to cash accounting where possible.

items.title: Add historical ROIC trending — multi-year view
items.note: Current scoring uses single period ROIC. Add multi-year ROIC chart (5-10 years) to Equity Scout deep dive. Shows consistency and trajectory of capital returns — key to identifying durable competitive advantage vs cyclical spike.

items.title: Total allocation view — combine direct + fund exposure by name
items.note: Holdings like ASML appear both as direct holding and inside ETFs/funds. Dashboard should show total economic exposure to each name across all vehicles. Exception: hedge funds isolating volatility should be treated separately.

items.title: Fund deep dive — separate format for ETFs and mutual funds
items.note: Holdings Explorer deep dive currently stock-focused. Add fund-specific deep dive showing: top holdings, historical performance, expense ratio, fund manager, fund summary. Different format than stock deep dive.

items.title: Punch list — add confirmation dialog to prevent accidental edits
items.note: Checkbox accidentally marked item as done and it disappeared from view. Add confirmation dialog before marking done or deleting. Also consider basic access control for commercial version to prevent unauthorized edits.

items.title: Retirement modeler — add cash/treasuries/equities allocator
items.note: Add allocation slider or input for cash, treasuries, and equities split. Feed into Monte Carlo simulation assumptions — different return/volatility profiles per asset class.

items.title: Retirement modeler — historical crisis scenario overlays
items.note: Add case study scenarios to retirement simulation: Japan-style stagnation, 1970s-80s oil crisis, Great Depression, GFC, Asian Financial Crisis, EU sovereign debt crisis. Show impact on portfolio and retirement outcome under each scenario.

items.note: We need to get rolling 12 month and YTD transactions and PY Capital GL

items.title: EDGAR — Build Company Facts API function in sec_utils.py
items.note: Add fetch_company_facts(ticker) to sec_utils.py using data.sec.gov/api/xbrl/companyfacts/. Returns structured dict of all XBRL concepts for a company. Must filter to most recent annual period per concept. Design data model to support full historical series (all years available), not just latest — this is the foundation for historical score trending (#9) and normalization. CIK lookup already exists in sec_utils.py.

items.title: EDGAR — Map Company Facts concepts to scoring engine fields
items.note: Define canonical field map: EDGAR US-GAAP concept → internal scoring field (e.g. NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities → op_cf). Handle concept aliases (companies use different XBRL tags for same economic item). Store map in edgar_concept_map.py. Must be designed so historical pulls use same map — no one-off patches per company.

items.title: EDGAR — Replace Polygon fundamentals in Equity Scout with EDGAR Company Facts
items.note: Wire fetch_company_facts() into fetch_fundamentals() in Equity Scout as primary source. Polygon becomes fallback only. Validate scoring output matches or improves on Polygon for 5 core holdings. Retain two-year history for YoY metrics. Verify interest expense, debt, and capex are sourced from correct XBRL concepts — these are the most likely to have multi-tag ambiguity.

items.title: EDGAR — Replace Polygon fundamentals in Dashboard with EDGAR Company Facts
items.note: Same swap as #49 but for Dashboard holdings fetch. ~15-20 tickers. Confirm performance is acceptable (sequential or light parallel). Remove Polygon dependency for fundamentals on this page entirely.

items.title: EDGAR — Build two-stage Market Screener (quality first, price second)
items.note: Stage 1: Pull fundamentals for ~500 tickers via EDGAR bulk company_facts.zip. Score on 65 quality points (ROIC, Debt/FCF, Gross Margin, Interest Coverage) — no price data needed. Filter to 42+ pt survivors (~40-80 tickers). Stage 2: Fetch price/market cap for survivors only via yfinance. Complete full 100-pt score. Design bulk ZIP download to cache within session. Rate limit: 10 req/sec for per-ticker fallback.

items.title: EDGAR — Historical full ingestion and normalization layer
items.note: The core normalization problem this item was after is solved: fetch_company_facts() in sec_utils.py now merges historical observations across ALL matching XBRL concept aliases for a field (instead of stopping at the first match), which fixed silent history truncation from tag transitions (e.g. the ASC 606 revenue tag change) and now gives accurate full-decade history live. This directly powers the Market Screener's 10-yr ROIC/FCF-margin averaging and passed real validation (Microsoft/Apple both show full 10-year history where they previously showed 3). REMAINING: the persistence/caching layer this item originally proposed (per-company normalized JSON files stored in GitHub, e.g. historical/COST.json) was not built -- all history is fetched live from EDGAR on every scan/lookup. Fine at current scan frequency and rate-limiting is already handled (shared rate limiter + retry), but worth revisiting if scan latency becomes a bigger pain point, or if point-in-time historical comparisons ACROSS scans (not just within one) become useful.

items.title: Smart ingestion — Phase 1: Manual entry form with template saving
items.note: Add manual entry mode to Equity Scout — toggle between API and Manual input. Structured form with labeled fields matching scoring engine inputs. On Calculate, saves values to company_templates.json in GitHub. Persists across sessions. Designed for companies where XBRL tagging is unreliable or missing (small caps, foreign filers). Template schema must include filing period and source label for future fuzzy matching in Phase 2.

items.title: Smart ingestion — Phase 2: Fuzzy match auto-suggestion from SEC text
items.note: Use rapidfuzz to match raw 10-K statement labels against saved templates from #53. Pre-populate manual entry form with best matches and confidence scores. User reviews, corrects, confirms — corrections update templates. Add rapidfuzz to requirements.txt. Requires sec_utils.py raw text fetch (already built). Design confidence score: exact match=1.0, fuzzy ratio 0.6-0.95, position bonus +0.10, value sanity check (within 3x prior year) +0.05.

items.title: Smart ingestion — Phase 3: Confidence threshold UI and auto-accept
items.note: Add slider: Auto-accept matches above [95%]. Fields above threshold auto-fill green, below threshold highlighted yellow for review. User corrections increment filing count in template, compounding accuracy over time. After 3-4 filings per company, most fields should reach auto-accept threshold with no manual review needed.

items.title: Smart ingestion — Phase 4: Cross-company industry pattern library
items.note: After 10-20 companies mapped, extract common label patterns by SIC code. New company auto-starts from industry template rather than blank form. Pairs with #36 (financial firm detection) and #37 (cyclical detection) — those SIC codes get flagged to skip auto-ingestion and route to manual review. Store industry patterns in industry_patterns.json in GitHub.

items.title: EDGAR — Validate EDGAR pages against Polygon for core holdings
items.note: Run 5 core holdings through both Equity Scout (Polygon) and Equity Scout EDGAR side by side. Confirm scores are consistent or improvements are explainable. Check: ROIC, Debt/FCF, Interest Coverage, Owner Earnings, Gross Margin. Document any discrepancies in concept mapping. Only retire Polygon pages after all 5 holdings validate cleanly. Market Screener EDGAR requires same validation before retiring 2_Market_Screener.py.

items.note: On the compare page of market screening, I want to be able to click on any of the financial metrics and see the historical trend of that metric across all companies in the compare.

items.title: Remove Long Squeeze macro overlay
items.note: Retired the Long Squeeze macro thesis (financial repression, passive index bubble) from Claude system prompt, suggested questions, and punch list seed text. App now evaluates businesses on fundamentals only, no baked-in market predictions.

items.title: Build diversification tool
items.note: When looking at my holdings I want to get a gauge of my diversification/risk.  In equity scout, it would be nice to see when the equity fits into the holdings diversification.  On market screener, it would be good to also include something that lets me see how investment in any of the selctions fits with my holdings and diversification strategy.  Probably need a top level tool to define what my diversification strategy is for assess in all other tools

items.title: Emerging Candidates track — Market Screener
items.note: Second, clearly-separate results bucket in the Market Screener for companies with 2-4 years of EDGAR history (below the main funnel's 5-year minimum) that still clear the SAME ROIC/FCF-margin thresholds as the main checklist AND show an improving/flat trend (not just a lucky average over a short window). Debt hurdle and dilution checks still apply as hard requirements. Purpose: surface businesses that look fundamentally solid but haven't had time to build a full 10-year track record, so they aren't simply invisible (current behavior: <5yr history = excluded outright, no flag, no visibility). Shown as a distinct "Emerging Candidates" section on the same Stage 1 results page, not mixed into the main survivor count. Pairs with #68 (tag-to-Watchlist + Watchlist page) so these can be revisited later as they accumulate history.

items.title: Tag-to-Watchlist + dedicated Watchlist page
items.note: Add a tag/star control on Market Screener result cards (main funnel survivors AND Emerging Candidates from #67) to add a ticker to a persistent Watchlist. Watchlist should live on its own page (not just session state) so tagged names persist across sessions/reboots — likely GitHub-backed via github_store.py, same pattern as the punch list and scan cache. This also satisfies/extends the existing open item #1 ("Watchlist — save top screener candidates") and #14 ("Watchlist persistence across sessions") — consider consolidating those into this item once scoped.

items.title: Market Screener scans block navigation / die on long runs
items.note: FIXED (pending live validation): Stage 1 runs in a background thread, decoupled from Streamlit script reruns. First attempt used a bare module-level dict for shared state, which does NOT survive Streamlit reruns (Streamlit re-executes the whole script from scratch each time -- that is the actual reason st.session_state exists). Corrected to use st.cache_resource, which returns the same object across reruns/sessions -- the right tool for this. Progress shown via an auto-refreshing @st.fragment(run_every=2) with a Cancel Scan button. GitHub cache save happens once, inside the worker thread, on completion. KNOWN LIMITATIONS: (1) cache_resource is process-global, not per-session/user -- fine for this single-user instance, needs to become session-keyed before any multi-user rollout (#15/#16); (2) does NOT survive an actual app restart/redeploy (a new git push, or Streamlit Cloud recycling the container) -- only survives in-app navigation/scrolling. A true fix for the redeploy case would need an out-of-process job queue -- separate, larger effort if it becomes a real pain point. NEEDS VALIDATION: not testable outside a live browser session from the dev sandbox -- confirm with a small scan (S&P 500) first, specifically testing navigate-away-and-back, before trusting it on a full 6,000+ ticker run.

items.title: Extend #36 alt scoring to Equity Scout/Compare/Dashboard + other financial subtypes
items.note: Follow-up to #36. The bank/insurer alt framework (evaluate_financial_firm_funnel/score_financial_firm_breakdown in sec_utils.py) is only wired into Market Screener today. Equity Scout, Compare Stocks, and Dashboard still score bank/insurer tickers under the standard ROIC/FCF-margin/Debt-FCF framework, which doesn't apply to them. Also: brokers/REITs/real estate/investment offices (financial_subtype "other_financial") still have no alt framework at all and are just excluded — would need their own metric sets (AUM/fee-based for brokers, FFO-based for REITs). Also add monoline mortgage/credit/financial-guaranty insurers (SIC 6351 — MGIC, Radian, Essent, NMI, Enact, etc., currently excluded as "other_financial") as a third alt-scoring subtype with its own metrics (loss ratio vs. risk-in-force, persistency, PMIERs-style capital adequacy) rather than the P&C combined-ratio framework, which doesn't fit their business model. UPDATE (this session): Extended #36 bank/insurer alt scoring to Dashboard, Equity Scout, and Compare Stocks (score-only update, per user's explicit choice -- NOT the pass/fail funnel checklist, just the continuous 0-100 score path). fetch_fundamentals_edgar() now surfaces financial_subtype + all 6 bank/insurer derived metrics + debt_to_cads. New sec_utils.score_financial_firm_display() wraps score_financial_firm_breakdown() with value/verdict/note per criterion so the richer display format (Equity Scout's style) is available to all three pages without duplicating formatting logic 3x. Equity Scout: branches to the alt scorer for bank/insurance subtypes, info banner replaces the old blanket 'reference only' warning. Compare Stocks: branches per-ticker in the score loop; Score Breakdown table now unions criterion names across ALL compared tickers (not just the first) so a mixed comparison (e.g. one bank + one industrial) doesn't silently drop rows. Dashboard: Score column branches to the alt scorer for holdings with a bank/insurance subtype, with a small 'Bank/Insurance scoring' caption under the badge; hold_verdict() (Signal column) also made subtype-aware -- substitutes ROE for ROIC and Equity/Assets for Debt/FCF, same swap the score itself makes, so Score and Signal don't contradict each other for the same holding. Tested with synthetic bank/insurer data (dual-hurdle scoring, display wrapper value/verdict/note formatting, non-financial subtype no-ops) -- all passing. other_financial subtypes (brokers/REITs/monoline mortgage insurers) are unaffected on these 3 pages -- still scored via the standard path, same as before, since only Market Screener has the skip-checkbox exclusion UX. Closing both #32 and #70.
